mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates directly increase discount rates for equity cash flows, suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of rising household savings (RSAFS) and stable federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) sustains consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth above the 100 index baseline indicates robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inventory accumulation phases or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in short-horizon walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising aggregate wages, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of observations or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing months or inflation fails to correlate with subsequent rate path.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of housing starts to unemployment rate determines market direction; when HOUST exceeds 1250 while UNRATE stays above 3.5%, it signals a fragile labor-housing dynamic that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect is statistically insignificant or signal predicts positive returns in >50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% annualized confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails during financial stress episodes or forward returns are negative in >55% of high-GDP quarters.
